km.Devon, England, United Kingdom: ដិវ៉ុន, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស, uណៃteត គីnហ្គដុម

zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger de la Peyre or Perry於1250年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親William de la Peyre or Perry30歲，母親Lady William de la Peyre26歲。他和Lady Roger de la Peyre至少有1個兒子和1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger de la Peyre or Perry于1250年出生在英国英格兰戴文时，他的父亲William de la Peyre or Perry30岁，母亲Lady William de la Peyre26岁。他和Lady Roger de la Peyre至少有1个儿子和1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Peyre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Hans.
South German: variant of   Beyer   or   Bayer  .

variants.name: Beyer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Otto, Erwin, Ewald, Klaus, Alois, Arno, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz, Theodor.
German, Scandinavian, English, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Bayer  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland.
Dutch: variant of Beijer, a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: Perry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Herry ‘son of Herry’, a variant of the personal name Harry (see Harris ).

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a pear tree (Middle English piri(e), peri(e), puri(e), Old English pirige, pyrige), or a habitational name for someone who owned or came from a place so named. This surname and a number of variants have been established in Ireland since the 17th century.

variants.name: Peery
meanings: Irish: variant of   Perry  , taken to Ireland at the time of thePlantation of Ulster and in the Cromwellian period.

variants.name: Pear
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dov.
English: topographic name from residence by a pear tree (Middle English pere, Old English peru, pere). Compare Perry .
English: nickname from Middle English pere, Old French per, peer ‘peer or paragon’, also ‘companion’.

variants.name: Peary
meanings: English (mainly northern): variant of   Perry  .

History: Robert E. Peary (1856–1920) was a U.S.-born explorer, who in 1909 led the first successful expedition to the North Pole. Peary Land in the north of Greenland is named after him, as he explored there in 1892 and 1900.

variants.name: Perrie
meanings: Scottish: variant of Perry 2. Alternatively, perhaps from the Old French personal name Pierre; see Pierce .

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Perryman
meanings: English (Devon): elaborated form of Perry , with Middle English man, probably denoting one who lived near a pear tree; see Pearman .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger de la Peyre or Perry was born in 1250, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, William de la Peyre or Perry, was 30 and his mother, Lady William de la Peyre, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Lady Roger de la Peyre.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger de la Peyre or Perry est né en 1250, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de la Peyre or Perry, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady William de la Peyre, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Lady Roger de la Peyre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger de la Peyre or Perry 1250 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de la Peyre or Perry 30 und seine Mutter Lady William de la Peyre 26. Er hatte mit Lady Roger de la Peyre mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de la Peyre or Perry è nato il nel 1250 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William de la Peyre or Perry, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady William de la Peyre, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Lady Roger de la Peyre.

ja.Devon, England, United Kingdom: イギリスイギリスデボン
ja.lifeSummary: Roger de la Peyre or Perryが1250年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父William de la Peyre or Perryは30歳，母Lady William de la Peyreは26歳であった。彼はLady Roger de la Peyreとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger de la Peyre or Perry(이)가 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de la Peyre or Perry(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady William de la Peyre(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Lady Roger de la Peyre(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger de la Peyre or Perry nasceu em 1250, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William de la Peyre or Perry, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady William de la Peyre, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Lady Roger de la Peyre.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger de la Peyre or Perry родился в 1250 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William de la Peyre or Perry было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady William de la Peyre, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Lady Roger de la Peyre.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger de la Peyre or Perry nació en 1250, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William de la Peyre or Perry, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady William de la Peyre, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija con Lady Roger de la Peyre.
